heroHeadline: US Levies $20M in Bounties on Chapitos Brothers; 75 Cartel Associates Hit with Visa Bans
heroSubtitle: 635 days since Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada's capture — April 16: US doubles bounty on El Mochomito to $10M, matching Iván Archivaldo's reward ($20M combined for both at-large Chapitos brothers); April 20: Secretary Rubio imposes visa restrictions on 75 Sinaloa Cartel-linked individuals under EO 14059. EDNY sentencing for El Mayo postponed a second time to May 18, 2026; Ovidio Guzmán López sentencing in Chicago pushed to July 27, 2026. Civil war at 589 days: La Mayiza controls ~90% of former Chapitos territory; 2,415+ conflict homicides, 9,000+ families displaced. El Güero Palma, Sinaloa Cartel co-founder, ordered medical care by federal judge at Altiplano prison (Apr 18).
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP, Infobae, La Jornada, CBC News, Excelsior, El Diario del Noroeste, White House ONDCP, DOJ, El Informador, El Imparcial, Latin Times, Zeta Tijuana, NBC Los Angeles, KTLA, Washington Times, NBC Chicago, UPI, US State Department
